UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY MURPHY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS DENIED IN PART, AND SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING IS REQUIRED. MOTION IS DENIED TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO RE-ALLEGE CAUSES OF ACTION TO WHICH DEMURRER WAS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. MOTION IS ALSO DENIED AS TO ANY ALLEGATIONS REGARDING DEFENDANT DAETWYLER IN LIGHT OF RULING ON SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO FILE AND SERVE BY PERSONAL OR ELECTRONIC SERVICE NO LATER THAN SEPTEMBER 17, 2013 A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF AND DECLARATION (TOTAL PAGES, EXCLUDING EXHIBITS, NOT TO EXCEED 15) SPECIFYING ALL REMAINING PROPOSED AMENDMENTS/SUPPLEMENTAL ALLEGATIONS, IF ANY. PLAINTIFF IS ALSO ORDERED TO ADDRESS THE PROPRIETY OF SUPPLEMENTING FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WHERE THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT HAS BEEN FILED AND IS THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS SHALL FILE AND SERVE THEIR SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSE (NOT TO EXCEED 15 PAGES) NO LATER THAN SEPTEMBER 23, 2013. HEARING IS SET FOR SEPTEMBER 25, 2013 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 501. SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING AND RESPONSES ARE DUE BEFORE SEPTEMBER 25, 2013. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: HARRY DORFMAN, CLERK: MARS GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Craig Brinckerhoff (415-981-5411) apperaing for Defendant and moving party / Plaintiff Timothy Murphy (415-452-6216) appearing in Pro Per and opposing party.

Argued - The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: KLESTOFF AND WEST & PRASZKER REALTY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is
1) SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to 4th, 5th, 6th, 7th and 8th causes of action added to First Amended Complaint without leave of Court and in excess of the scope of permitted amendment per Order sustaining Demurrer to 8/20/12 Complaint with leave to Amend. Contrary to Plaintiff's assertion, in ruling on Defendants' demurrer the Court considered only challenges to causes of action alleged in the complaint and not the whole universe of potential unalleged counts that Plaintiff might have against Defendants; the Court granted Plaintiff a leave to amend to address those challenges/issues. Harris v. Wachovia (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 1018.
2) SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to 1st cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotion Distress to allege outrageous conduct exceeding all bounds of decency, not mere insults, indignities, threats, annoyances, petty oppression or other trivialities. CACI 1602.
3) OVERRULED as to 2nd and 9th causes of action. Whether or not repairs were "necessary" is an issue of fact not properly raised on demurrer.
4) SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to 3rd cause of action to allege facts showing violation(s) of CCC 1940.2(a)(1) [facts showing violation of Penal Code 484(a)].
Plaintiff shall have 20 days from the Notice of Entry of Order to file an amended complaint. Prevailing Party to Prepare Order. Order for the same signed in open court. Judge: Harry Dorfman, Court reporter: Rose Miller (Csr #:5041, Telephone #: 925-200-1389), Clerk: N. Bautista, (D501)
